Taxonomy_y: Eukaryota; Metazoa; Ecdysozoa; Arthropoda; Hexapoda; Insecta; Pterygota; Neoptera; Endopterygota; Lepidoptera; Glossata; Ditrysia; Tineoidea; Gracillariidae; Phyllonorycter
Assembly: The assembly ilPhyUlic4.1 is based on 72x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project~(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, the primary assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. Z chromosome identified based on BUSCO gene painting with ancestral Merian elements ( https://doi.org/10.1038/s41559-024-02329-4).PacBio and Hi-C data were generated from different, unsexed individuals. In the haploid assembly, the Z chromosome appears at approximately half the PacBio read coverage. In addition, numerous unlocalised scaffolds show half coverage and lack Hi-C signal. These scaffolds likely represent fragmented W chromosome sequences, consistent with the PacBio data originating from a male and the Hi-C data from a female.

Assembly: The assembly ihHarThor2.1 is based on 29x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project~(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. A manually phased assembly (based on HiC signal) was generated from a jointly curated primary and alt assembly. Finally, the primary assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. X chromosome identified based on synteny with Pilophorus perplexus (GCA_955831195.1). Chromosome 5 has a heterozygous inversion between 12.2 Mb and 23.7 Mb.

Assembly: The assembly idGymViri1.1 is based on 47x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project~(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, retained haplotig separation with purge_dups, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, the primary assembly was analysed and manually improved using rapid curation. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. Chromosome X was assigned based on read coverage statistics. The species appears to be XO. The following regions of the assembly are of uncertain order and orientation: Chromosome 1 - 60.5 - 69.5 Mbp, Chromosome 5 - 43.4 - 52.6 Mbp.

Assembly: The assembly ucDunPrim2.2 is based on 40x PacBio data, 10X Genomics Chromium data, and Arima Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project (https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, retained haplotig separation with purge_dups, short-read polishing using FreeBayes-called variants from 10X Genomics Chromium reads aligned with LongRanger, and Hi-C based scaffolding with SALSA2. The mitochondrial and chloroplast genomes were assembled using MBG from PacBio HiFi reads mapping to related genomes. A representative circular sequence was selected for each from the graph based on read coverage. Finally, the primary assembly was analysed and manually improved using rapid curation. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size.
